page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 7T83 physical cores operating at a base frequency of 2.55 GHz, with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high resource density of 8.0 GB of memory per core. It operates on the KVM hypervisor, lacks local storage, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance characteristics across multiple domains. The instance achieves top-tier results in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, alongside strong passmark CPU encryption and extended instruction scores. Memory performance is highly efficient for cached operations, registering a strong read-only small block bandwidth of 4144.0 GB/sec, though uncached large-block read and write speeds fall into the average range. Database and key-value store capabilities are highlighted by Redis SET operations reaching 61.54 million operations per second. For machine learning workloads, the instance demonstrates strong prompt processing and text generation speeds on small LLM models, though performance scales down to average levels on medium 7B models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.r7a.16xlarge offers high memory density and dedicated CPU allocation, making it a viable choice for memory-bound enterprise applications. However, the lack of bundled local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring external block storage attachment for persistent data. This configuration is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, data analytics, and memory-heavy enterprise applications.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7a.16xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 7T83 physical cores running at 2.55 GHz and 512.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, it provides 8.0 GB of memory per core and a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps, though it lacks local storage. Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities in multi-core CPU execution, cached memory bandwidth, and Redis SET operations, alongside average uncached memory bandwidth and database operations. This high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes the instance highly efficient for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and data analytics, though users must trade off the lack of local storage by utilizing external block storage.

tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high memory density for demanding database and enterprise workloads.

512.0 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB of memory per core
Strong performance in multi-core CPU benchmarks and Redis operations
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on KVM hypervisor
No local storage, requiring external block storage solutions
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and data analytics
